commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Google Chat 403 upload-auth fallback-to-text-link handling for outbound adapter and monitor reply-delivery media sends, exports an upload-auth failure helper, and adds focused Vitest coverage.

PR surface: Source +60, Tests +188. Total +248 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows service-account chat.bot auth reaches the media upload path, and the message-action path still calls uploadGoogleChatAttachment directly before sending; no live Google Chat credential run was available here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused automated repair can complete the missing message-action fallback and tests without product, config, SDK, or security decisions.

target.title: fix(googlechat): fall back to text link when remote media upload fails with 403
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #89430 reports that Google Chat agents using app-auth (service-account, `chat.bot` scope) cannot deliver replies that include image or file attachments. The official Google Chat API contract requires user auth for `media.upload`; app-auth receives a 403. Both the outbound adapter and the monitor reply delivery path attempted the upload, propagated or dropped the 403, and silently discarded the agent reply.
- **Root Cause**: Two delivery paths unconditionally call `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` with no fallback. `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` wrapped the entire buffer-load + upload + `sendGoogleChatMessage` chain in one try/catch that logged the error; `channel.adapters.ts` propagated the upload error up without any catch. `withGoogleChatResponse` throws `"Google Chat upload 403: …"` for HTTP 403 on the upload endpoint (controlled by `errorPrefix: "Google Chat upload"` at `api.ts:228`), providing a precise prefix that distinguishes upload auth failures from `sendGoogleChatMessage` 403s (which use the default `"Google Chat API"` prefix).
- **Fix**: Export `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` from `api.ts` (matches `"Google Chat upload 403:"` exactly to avoid false positives) and apply a narrow try/catch around only `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` in both paths. When upload fails with this error and the media URL is a remote http(s) URL, fall back to sending the URL as a text link with the caption. Preserve the original always-resolving contract: `readRemoteMediaBuffer` and `sendGoogleChatMessage` each get their own catch blocks that log and swallow, keeping `sendMedia` always-resolving as before. Non-403 upload failures still log and return.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts` — export `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` (pure string check, no runtime deps).
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.adapters.ts` — import helper; wrap only `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` in try/catch; on 403 + remote URL send text link fallback.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor-reply-delivery.ts` — import helper; split `sendMedia` into separate try/catch blocks for buffer load, upload, and send to restore the original per-phase error contract; add 403 fallback on upload.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts` — 2 new tests: 403 fallback returns text-link result; non-403 re-throws.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` — 5 new tests: 403 with caption; 403 URL-only; non-403 logs error; buffer load failure logs error; sendGoogleChatMessage failure after successful upload logs error.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.ts` — delegates through the same `channel.adapters.ts` outbound adapter; adapter fix covers it without a separate change.
  - Upload behavior for non-403 errors is unchanged.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin SDK surface unchanged.
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure Google Chat with service-account app auth (`chat.bot` scope).
2. Send a message that includes a remote image or file URL as a media attachment.
3. **Before this PR**: `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` receives a 403; both paths log an error and drop the reply — no message is delivered.
4. **After this PR**: upload fails with `"Google Chat upload 403: …"`; `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` detects it; falls back to `sendGoogleChatMessage` with `[caption, mediaUrl].join("\n")` as text — the reply reaches the user as a link.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#89430)**: when `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` throws `"Google Chat upload 403: …"` and the media URL is a remote http(s) URL, both `channel.adapters.ts` and `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` now send a text-link fallback instead of dropping the reply.

**Real environment tested (Ubuntu Server Node 24, source-level — Vitest against production `deliverGoogleChatReply` and `googlechatOutboundAdapter.attachedResults.sendMedia` with mocked `uploadGoogleChatAttachment`)**: the 403 fallback tests call production functions with the mocked upload throwing the exact error format from `withGoogleChatResponse`, then assert on `sendGoogleChatMessage` call args and status sink. No live Google Chat credentials were available; the API contract (403 for `chat.bot` scope on `media.upload`) is documented and source-confirmed via `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts:59` and `extensions/googlechat/src/auth.ts:11`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` — 27 tests pass (20 pre-existing + 7 new). `pnpm exec oxlint` clean. `pnpm format:check` clean.

**Evidence after fix (Vitest output for the touched test files)**:

```
Tests  27 passed (27)
```

New tests cover: adapter 403 fallback returns text-link receipt; adapter non-403 re-throws; monitor 403 fallback with caption; monitor 403 URL-only; monitor non-403 logs error; monitor buffer-load failure logs error (original contract); monitor post-upload `sendGoogleChatMessage` failure logs error (original contract).

**Observed result after fix**: on a 403 upload error, `sendGoogleChatMessage` receives `"caption\nhttps://…"` or `"https://…"` with no `attachments` field; `statusSink` is called once on success; `runtime.error` is not called on the 403 path.

**What was not tested**: live Google Chat end-to-end with a real service-account credential.

**Repro confirmation**: the 403 fallback tests fail on the pre-patch tree and pass with the fix; the buffer-load and send-failure tests fail without the corrected try/catch scoping, confirming each catch block is independently required.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` fires on non-upload 403s. **Mitigation**: matches `"Google Chat upload 403:"` (set by `errorPrefix: "Google Chat upload"` at `api.ts:228`); `sendGoogleChatMessage` uses `"Google Chat API"` so its 403s never match.
- **Risk**: Text-link fallback fires for non-scope upload failures. **Mitigation**: gated on `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` (403 only) AND remote http(s) URL; size/network/other-status failures still log and return.
- **Risk**: Behavioral regression in `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` — buffer load or send failures no longer caught. **Mitigation**: each phase now has its own catch block matching the original always-resolving contract; regression tests cover both scenarios explicitly.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Google Chat plugin

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #89430
target.author: openperf
